Spot & flow Mar 20, 2026

Altcoins showing relative strength in a quiet tape

High-liquidity pairs with clean continuation and valid invalidation deserve the watchlist—avoid chasing thin books when breadth is mixed.

When majors chop, alts often rotate. The useful filter is not “which ticker is green,” but which names respect levels on volume you can actually trade.

  1. Prefer pairs where the last swing high/low is obvious and liquidity is not a ghost town.
  2. If breadth is mixed, reduce correlation risk: fewer concurrent alt longs, tighter caps on total heat.
  3. Treat relative strength as permission to stalk setups, not a mandate to click.

For live levels and timing, use the Signals page and VIP delivery—this note is framing only.
